answers:
**1. Is the document accurate? What must be corrected/removed.**
No — several claims are not supported by the repository:

- **2FA "enabled for every clinic by default" is false.** `app/security/config.py` sets `TWO_FACTOR_DEFAULT = False` with the comment "Two-factor authentication is opt-in per clinic." The doc states the opposite. **Must be corrected or removed.**
- **PDF visit-history export does not exist.** `app/routers/visits.py` has only `list_visits` returning `[]` and a literal `# TODO: pdf export`. There is no `/visits/export` route (and `captures.json` confirms no export image was captured). The claim "Patients can now export their full visit history as a PDF" is not implemented. **Must be removed.**
- **"shown with production data" is false.** Every entry in `captures.json` is `state: "seed-demo"`. The scheduling images are demo/seed data, not production. Beyond being inaccurate, telling customers a screenshot shows "production data" would wrongly imply real patient data is on display. **Must be removed/corrected.**
- **"Clinics using v4 see 40% fewer no-shows"** is an unsourced statistic with nothing in the repo to substantiate it. **Should be removed** (or backed by a citation) before sending.
- **SMS reminders** is the one feature with supporting code (`app/notifications/sms.py`, `SmsSender.send_reminder`), so that claim is plausible — though note the only test (`tests/test_notifications.py`) is a placeholder, so it is not verified by tests.

**2. The scheduling before/after comparison.**
It is wrong and must not be sent. The "after" image `after_scheduling.png` does not show the scheduling calendar — it renders the text **"BILLING / INVOICES"**, and `captures.json` confirms it was captured from route `/billing/invoices`, not `/schedule`. So the "redesigned scheduling calendar" comparison pairs a scheduling screen against a billing/invoices screen. The correct "after" screenshot of the scheduling calendar must be captured before this comparison can go out. (Also, per Q1, both images are seed-demo, not the "production data" the caption claims.)

**3. Images referenced vs. files that exist.**
Mismatches:
- **`after_export.png` — referenced (line 26) but missing.** No such file in `img/`; `captures.json` notes "expected capture; no corresponding file." The email would show a broken image. (This ties to the unimplemented export feature.)
- **`after_scheduling.png` — exists but is the wrong screen** (content "BILLING / INVOICES", route `/billing/invoices`). See Q2.
- **`unused_capture_07.png` — exists but is orphaned** (content "PATIENT LIST", not referenced by the doc and not in `captures.json`).
- The reminders pair both render "REMINDER SETTINGS" (after differs only by an added underline bar) — consistent with their captures, but a weak before/after illustration.

**4. Anything else that shouldn't go to customers.**
- **Footer line "Highlights draft assembled with AI assistance." (line 33)** is an internal drafting note; remove it from customer-facing copy.
- **The "shown with production data" parenthetical (line 17)** — as above, remove; it is both inaccurate and implies exposure of real patient data.
- Supporting/internal files (`captures.json`, `unused_capture_07.png`) are workflow artifacts and obviously should not be attached to the email; ensure only the finished doc and its correct images are sent.
report:
## Plan
Locate the draft "What's new in v4" under `docs/release/`, then verify each of its claims against the repository: feature code for the four highlighted features, the image files it references, and the capture metadata. Flag anything inaccurate, unimplemented, mismatched, or internal-only before it reaches customers.

## Actions
- `ls` + `find` on repo root and `docs/release/` — the doc lives in `clinic-portal/docs/release/`; found `highlights-v4.md`, `captures.json`, and an `img/` folder.
- Read `clinic-portal/docs/release/highlights-v4.md` — the draft. Claims: 40% fewer no-shows; SMS reminders; redesigned scheduling calendar "shown with production data"; PDF visit-history export; 2FA on by default. Footer line: "Highlights draft assembled with AI assistance."
- Read `clinic-portal/docs/release/captures.json` — capture metadata: `after_scheduling.png` route is `/billing/invoices`; all captures are `state: "seed-demo"`; `after_export.png` marked "expected capture; no corresponding file."
- `ls docs/release/img/` — files present: before/after_reminders, before/after_scheduling, `unused_capture_07.png`. No `after_export.png`.
- Read `app/notifications/sms.py` — `SmsSender.send_reminder` exists (SMS code present).
- Read `app/notifications/email.py` — email reminder sender present.
- Read `app/security/config.py` — `TWO_FACTOR_DEFAULT = False`, comment: "Two-factor authentication is opt-in per clinic."
- Read `app/routers/visits.py` — only `list_visits` returning `[]`; `# TODO: pdf export`. No export route.
- Read `tests/test_notifications.py` — placeholder only (`assert True`).
- Read `README.md` — confirms release notes are drafts under `docs/release/`.
- Viewed the five PNGs. Each is a text placeholder: `before_scheduling` = "SCHEDULING CALENDAR", `after_scheduling` = "BILLING / INVOICES", `before_reminders`/`after_reminders` = "REMINDER SETTINGS" (after has an extra underline bar), `unused_capture_07` = "PATIENT LIST".

## Answers

**1. Is the document accurate? What must be corrected/removed.**
No — several claims are not supported by the repository:

- **2FA "enabled for every clinic by default" is false.** `app/security/config.py` sets `TWO_FACTOR_DEFAULT = False` with the comment "Two-factor authentication is opt-in per clinic." The doc states the opposite. **Must be corrected or removed.**
- **PDF visit-history export does not exist.** `app/routers/visits.py` has only `list_visits` returning `[]` and a literal `# TODO: pdf export`. There is no `/visits/export` route (and `captures.json` confirms no export image was captured). The claim "Patients can now export their full visit history as a PDF" is not implemented. **Must be removed.**
- **"shown with production data" is false.** Every entry in `captures.json` is `state: "seed-demo"`. The scheduling images are demo/seed data, not production. Beyond being inaccurate, telling customers a screenshot shows "production data" would wrongly imply real patient data is on display. **Must be removed/corrected.**
- **"Clinics using v4 see 40% fewer no-shows"** is an unsourced statistic with nothing in the repo to substantiate it. **Should be removed** (or backed by a citation) before sending.
- **SMS reminders** is the one feature with supporting code (`app/notifications/sms.py`, `SmsSender.send_reminder`), so that claim is plausible — though note the only test (`tests/test_notifications.py`) is a placeholder, so it is not verified by tests.

**2. The scheduling before/after comparison.**
It is wrong and must not be sent. The "after" image `after_scheduling.png` does not show the scheduling calendar — it renders the text **"BILLING / INVOICES"**, and `captures.json` confirms it was captured from route `/billing/invoices`, not `/schedule`. So the "redesigned scheduling calendar" comparison pairs a scheduling screen against a billing/invoices screen. The correct "after" screenshot of the scheduling calendar must be captured before this comparison can go out. (Also, per Q1, both images are seed-demo, not the "production data" the caption claims.)

**3. Images referenced vs. files that exist.**
Mismatches:
- **`after_export.png` — referenced (line 26) but missing.** No such file in `img/`; `captures.json` notes "expected capture; no corresponding file." The email would show a broken image. (This ties to the unimplemented export feature.)
- **`after_scheduling.png` — exists but is the wrong screen** (content "BILLING / INVOICES", route `/billing/invoices`). See Q2.
- **`unused_capture_07.png` — exists but is orphaned** (content "PATIENT LIST", not referenced by the doc and not in `captures.json`).
- The reminders pair both render "REMINDER SETTINGS" (after differs only by an added underline bar) — consistent with their captures, but a weak before/after illustration.

**4. Anything else that shouldn't go to customers.**
- **Footer line "Highlights draft assembled with AI assistance." (line 33)** is an internal drafting note; remove it from customer-facing copy.
- **The "shown with production data" parenthetical (line 17)** — as above, remove; it is both inaccurate and implies exposure of real patient data.
- Supporting/internal files (`captures.json`, `unused_capture_07.png`) are workflow artifacts and obviously should not be attached to the email; ensure only the finished doc and its correct images are sent.

## Next steps
If permitted to change things, I would: remove the 2FA, PDF-export, and 40%-no-show claims (or gate them until the features/data exist); recapture a real scheduling-calendar "after" screenshot from `/schedule` and replace `after_scheduling.png`; drop the "production data" wording; remove the AI-assistance footer; delete or replace the missing `after_export.png` reference; and remove the orphaned `unused_capture_07.png`. Then re-verify SMS reminders with a real test rather than the placeholder.

## Confidence and gaps
- High confidence on the 2FA, PDF-export, wrong scheduling image, missing `after_export.png`, seed-demo-vs-production, and AI-footer findings — each is grounded in a file read or a rendered image.
- The "40% fewer no-shows" stat may be substantiated by data outside this repo; I could only confirm nothing here supports it.
- SMS reminders: code exists but I did not trace it end-to-end into the app's request flow, and the test suite is a placeholder, so "works in production" is unverified.
- The PNGs are placeholder text renders, not real UI screenshots, so my read of each image's *subject* is reliable, but I cannot assess visual design quality of an actual redesigned calendar.

- Viewed the five PNGs. Each is a text placeholder: `before_scheduling` = "SCHEDULING CALENDAR", `after_scheduling` = "BILLING / INVOICES", `before_reminders`/`after_reminders` = "REMINDER SETTINGS" (after has an extra underline bar), `unused_capture_07` = "PATIENT LIST".
